patch:
commit 8e8467d9d8033e22a98aec3bc20969bd3a59ab28
Author: Sanjay Patel <spatel@rotateright.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 19 10:33:30 2023 -0500

    [InstCombine] canonicalize "extract lowest set bit" away from cttz intrinsic
    
    1 << (cttz X) --> -X & X
    https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/qv3E9e
    
    This creates an extra use of the input value, so that's generally
    not preferred, but there are advantages to this direction:
    1. 'negate' and 'and' allow for better analysis than 'cttz'.
    2. This is more likely to induce follow-on transforms (in the
       example from issue #60801, we'll get the decrement pattern).
    3. The more basic ALU ops are more likely to result in better
       codegen across a variety of targets.
    
    This won't solve the motivating bugs (see issue #60799) because
    we do not recognize the redundant icmp+sel, and the x86 backend
    may not have the pattern-matching to produce the optimal BMI
    instructions.
    
    Differential Revision: https://reviews.llvm.org/D144329

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineShifts.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineShifts.cpp
index 3dd8414e950a..f8d4e05e9eb8 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineShifts.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineShifts.cpp
@@ -1150,6 +1150,14 @@ Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitShl(BinaryOperator &I) {
       return BinaryOperator::CreateLShr(
           ConstantInt::get(Ty, APInt::getSignMask(BitWidth)), X);
 
+    // Canonicalize "extract lowest set bit" using cttz to and-with-negate:
+    // 1 << (cttz X) --> -X & X
+    if (match(Op1,
+              m_OneUse(m_Intrinsic<Intrinsic::cttz>(m_Value(X), m_Value())))) {
+      Value *NegX = Builder.CreateNeg(X, "neg");
+      return BinaryOperator::CreateAnd(NegX, X);
+    }
+
     // The only way to shift out the 1 is with an over-shift, so that would
     // be poison with or without "nuw". Undef is excluded because (undef << X)
     // is not undef (it is zero).

tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare <2 x i8> @llvm.cttz.v2i8(<2 x i8>, i1 immarg) #0

define <2 x i8> @shl1_cttz_vec(<2 x i8> %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @shl1_cttz_vec(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[NEG:%.*]] = sub <2 x i8> zeroinitializer, [[X:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SHL:%.*]] = and <2 x i8> [[NEG]], [[X]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <2 x i8> [[SHL]]
  %tz = call <2 x i8> @llvm.cttz.v2i8(<2 x i8> %x, i1 false)
  %shl = shl <2 x i8> <i8 1, i8 1>, %tz
  ret <2 x i8> %shl
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

declare void @use_i32(i32)

define i32 @ashr_sdiv_extra_use(i32 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @ashr_sdiv_extra_use(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[D:%.*]] = sdiv i32 [[X:%.*]], 42
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use_i32(i32 [[D]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = icmp slt i32 [[X]], -41
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = sext i1 [[TMP1]] to i32
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[R]]
  %d = sdiv i32 %x, 42
  call void @use_i32(i32 %d)
  %r = ashr i32 %d, 31
  ret i32 %r
}
tests.source_program:
declare void @use_i32(i32)

define i32 @ashr_sdiv_extra_use(i32 %x) {
  %d = sdiv i32 %x, 42
  call void @use_i32(i32 %d)
  %r = ashr i32 %d, 31
  ret i32 %r
}

tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32, i1 immarg) #0

define i32 @shl2_cttz(i32 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @shl2_cttz(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TZ:%.*]] = call i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32 [[X:%.*]], i1 true), !range [[RNG0]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SHL:%.*]] = shl i32 2, [[TZ]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[SHL]]
  %tz = call i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32 %x, i1 true)
  %shl = shl i32 2, %tz
  ret i32 %shl
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

declare void @use_i32(i32)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32, i1 immarg) #0

define i32 @shl1_cttz_extra_use(i32 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @shl1_cttz_extra_use(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TZ:%.*]] = call i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32 [[X:%.*]], i1 false), !range [[RNG0:![0-9]+]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use_i32(i32 [[TZ]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SHL:%.*]] = shl nuw i32 1, [[TZ]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[SHL]]
  %tz = call i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32 %x, i1 false)
  call void @use_i32(i32 %tz)
  %shl = shl i32 1, %tz
  ret i32 %shl
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare <2 x i8> @llvm.cttz.v2i8(<2 x i8>, i1 immarg) #0

define <2 x i8> @shl1_cttz_vec_poison(<2 x i8> %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @shl1_cttz_vec_poison(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[NEG:%.*]] = sub <2 x i8> zeroinitializer, [[X:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SHL:%.*]] = and <2 x i8> [[NEG]], [[X]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <2 x i8> [[SHL]]
  %tz = call <2 x i8> @llvm.cttz.v2i8(<2 x i8> %x, i1 false)
  %shl = shl <2 x i8> <i8 1, i8 poison>, %tz
  ret <2 x i8> %shl
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32, i1 immarg) #0

define i32 @shl1_cttz(i32 %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @shl1_cttz(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[NEG:%.*]] = sub i32 0, [[X:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SHL:%.*]] = and i32 [[NEG]], [[X]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[SHL]]
  %tz = call i32 @llvm.cttz.i32(i32 %x, i1 true)
  %shl = shl i32 1, %tz
  ret i32 %shl
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

issue.title: Simplify "X ? 1U << std::countr_zero(X) : 0" to "X & -X"
issue.author: kazutakahirata

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
We don't need to match the guard for a zero input to convert the cttz+shl alone:
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/qv3E9e

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
Removing the guard of the zero input is safe here and in #60801 because there's no chance for poison to leak through where it did not in the original code. However, it is not safe in general:
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/Cr8PL2

So we do not get the simplification with the general transform in "simplifyWithOpReplaced".
Three potential options to fix this:
1. There's a possible missed generalization with "simplifyWithOpReplaced" - we don't check "isGuaranteedNotToBeUndefOrPoison" for the false value of the select when trying the replacement. In the minimal source example here, we have 'noundef' on the input argument, so that would probably work, but it's not a universal fix.
2. We can add the "and" sequences of these 2 bugs to the approved list of patterns in "simplifyWithOpReplaced" ( https://reviews.llvm.org/D99027 ).
3. We can freeze the other operand of the "and" as part of a specialized pattern match: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/s_PbhD (we have added something like that already for "mul" patterns).

@nikic - any preference?

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: I think I'd go for option 2 for now, and then maybe add 3 later if we have other motivating cases.

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
Patch proposal that generalizes option 2:
https://reviews.llvm.org/D144493

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
The IR should be ideal now, and we get the ideal x86 asm too for the "-march" options that I tried. 

A more complicated example might still not be ideal (I noticed that x86 codegen didn't always produce the expected BMI asm with the cmp+select still in place). Please file another issue if you find more problems.
